run_metadata.coverage_summary: Attempted the full source universe. Direct page retrieval remained fully blocked this run: WebFetch returned HTTP 403 Forbidden for every external URL tested (tenders.gov.au, buy.nsw, vendorpanel.com.au, and even aggregator hosts such as govmarket.com.au), so no tender detail page, listing page, feed/API, or specification document could be opened. All capture was therefore from WebSearch result titles/snippets, cross-checked across multiple queries. Tier 1 (AusTender + all eight state/territory portals) probed via search; Tier 2 sampled (local government via VendorPanel and individual council pages, universities, water GBEs, Transport for NSW); Tier 3 corporate check performed (SAP Ariba Discovery / SAP Business Network). Consistent with prior runs, search snippets frequently recycled already-closed opportunities as if current, so every candidate close date was cross-verified: Sydney Water WS4605137146 (closed 14 Oct 2025), Snowy Monaro plant & equipment hire panel (closed 11 Aug 2025), and University of Melbourne Refurbishment Contractor Panel (closed 10 Apr 2026) were all discarded as stale. Three genuinely current, buyer-originated opportunities were verified: the Department of Defence ESTS4000PH6 EOI (published 6 Jul 2026, closes 17 Aug 2026 - well corroborated with scope, evaluation criteria and contact), the Clean Energy Regulator ICT-capability REOI PIN_2026_0334 (a current AusTender ATM last updated 21 Jul 2026), and a Transport for NSW Mittagong steel-safety-barrier RFT (scope corroborated across two snippets; cited close 3 Aug 2026 unverified). Three genuinely new buyer-originated sources were recorded (DECnet Defence-estate channel, Snowy Monaro Regional Council, Penrith City Council). Zero tenders were fabricated.
gaps: WebFetch returned HTTP 403 Forbidden for every external URL this run (verified on tenders.gov.au, buy.nsw, vendorpanel.com.au and govmarket.com.au). The proxy status endpoint reported no relay failures, so the 403s are destination-side bot protection, not an egress-policy denial. No detail page, listing page, RSS/API endpoint or specification document could be retrieved directly; all detail was limited to WebSearch titles/snippets.

Clean Energy Regulator ICT-capability REOI (AusTender PIN_2026_0334): confirmed as a current AusTender ATM last updated 21 Jul 2026, but the closing date, publish date and value could not be verified from search snippets (the '30 September 2026' date came from the query, not a confirmed source) and the detail page returned 403. Recorded at headline level, pending a detail pass.

Transport for NSW 'Mittagong road network steel safety barriers' RFT: scope corroborated across two buy.nsw search snippets, but no RFT ID was recoverable and the cited 3 August 2026 closing date appeared in only one snippet and could not be verified (detail page 403). Recorded at headline level, pending a detail pass.

Search snippets again recycled closed/stale opportunities as if current. Confirmed stale and NOT recorded: Sydney Water WS4605137146 Sewer Network Repairs & Conduit Condition Inspection (closed 14 Oct 2025); Snowy Monaro Regional Council Plant & Equipment Hire Panel (closed 11 Aug 2025); University of Melbourne Refurbishment Contractor Panel 2026 EOI (closed 10 Apr 2026).

AusTender surfaced several other current 2026 ATM IDs by reference only (e.g. CFI-20260825, EWPNOTICE 01/2026, MSD/RFT/55619/1, DHAPROC26/750) with no publicly-visible title/buyer/close date in snippets; not recorded because they could not be titled or dated without fabrication. Deferred to a run with working page retrieval.

Tier-1 state/territory portals (buy.nsw/NSW eTendering, Buying for Victoria, QTenders/supply.qld, Tenders WA, SA Tenders, Tenders Tasmania, NT QTOL, ACT Tenders): reachable as portals via search but, apart from the two NSW items above, no individual open tender could be confirmed as newly published in the last 7 days at a trustworthy level of detail (403 wall + unreliable snippets).

Tier-3 corporate (SAP Ariba Discovery / SAP Business Network, plus BHP, Rio Tinto, Fortescue, Woodside, Santos, Coles, Woolworths, Qantas, banks, telcos): checked as required. No publicly-advertised Australian RFx was visible without a supplier login; corporate opportunities remain registration-gated or invitation-only, so none were captured as tenders. The relevant portals are already recorded in prior runs' discovered_sources.
recommended_seed_additions: DECnet - Defence Estate Community (https://decnet.com.au) - public notice/community channel where the Department of Defence and its managing contractors publish estate capital-works ATMs/EOIs; useful for construction/facilities opportunities not obvious from AusTender rows.
Snowy Monaro Regional Council (NSW) tenders - https://www.snowymonaro.nsw.gov.au (quotes/panels via VendorPanel; procurement contact 1300 345 345, council@smrc.nsw.gov.au).

tenders.title: ESTS4000PH6 - Facilities to Support Hobart Class Destroyer (DDG) Aegis Combat System Enhancement
tenders.category: Construction and facilities / Defence estate works
tenders.description: Multi-stage Expression of Interest to identify a designer / delivery team for facilities supporting SEA 4000 Phase 6 (the Hobart Class Destroyer Aegis Combat System Enhancement). Works are located in New South Wales and South Australia.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Design and delivery of new and refurbished training and working facilities to support the Hobart Class Destroyer Aegis Combat System Enhancement, including capital build and infrastructure works at Navy training establishments.
key_deliverables: Construction of new and refurbished training facilities at the Navy Training System
New/expanded working accommodation and supporting infrastructure
tenders.scope_of_work: New infrastructure and capital build facilities to support SEA 4000 Phase 6, including construction of new and refurbished training facilities, modified and expanded training facilities, and working accommodation across NSW and SA sites.
tenders.eligibility: Open to all (multi-stage EOI; respondents shortlisted from Stage 1)
tenders.submission_method: Via AusTender; EOI managed on behalf of Defence by RPS Consulting
tenders.delivery_location: New South Wales (Sydney) and South Australia
tenders.detail_source: DECnet ATM listing for ESTS4000PH6 and AusTender, surfaced via WebSearch (detail pages themselves returned HTTP 403 and were not directly opened)
tenders.reasoning: Discovered while searching AusTender/Defence estate EOIs; the full listing (dates, scope, evaluation criteria, contact) is published on DECnet, the buyer-adjacent Defence estate community channel, and corroborated across two independent searches. Buyer-originated (Department of Defence via managing contractor RPS Consulting).
tenders.notes: Detail extracted from publicly-visible DECnet/AusTender listing text via web search; the AusTender/DECnet detail pages and EOI documents returned HTTP 403 and could not be opened directly. AusTender ATM ID not confirmed. One loose search snippet cited an indicative value of ~AUD 60,000,000; not corroborated in the dedicated search, so value left null.

tenders.title: Request for Expressions of Interest - ICT Capability to Support Regulatory and Registry Functions
tenders.description: Stage 1 Request for Expressions of Interest (two-stage procurement) seeking suppliers with experience and capability to support the Clean Energy Regulator's current and future regulatory and registry functions through ICT capability. The REOI is used to understand market capability, solution approaches and implementation considerations, and to shortlist respondents for a subsequent Stage 2 Proof of Concept (a Request for Quote to design and deliver a PoC).
tenders.deliverables_summary: Stage 1 seeks market responses (capability, solution approach, implementation considerations) rather than a delivered good/service; shortlisted respondents may later be invited to design and deliver a proof-of-concept ICT capability supporting regulatory and registry functions.
tenders.eligibility: Open to all suppliers with relevant ICT experience and capability (Stage 1 REOI; shortlisting for Stage 2 PoC RFQ)
tenders.reasoning: Discovered via AusTender search for current ICT/EOI approaches; confirmed as a current AusTender ATM (PIN_2026_0334) for the Clean Energy Regulator, last updated 21 Jul 2026 per search snippets. Buyer-originated (Commonwealth agency on AusTender).
tenders.notes: Headline-level only. Closing date, publish date and value could not be verified - the AusTender detail page returned HTTP 403 and the '30 September 2026' date came from the search query rather than a confirmed source. Pending a detail pass.

tenders.title: Inspection, Repair and Replacement of Steel Safety Barriers - Mittagong Road Network
tenders.description: Request for Tender for works comprising the inspection, repair and replacement of damaged steel safety barriers - including guardrail and wire rope safety barrier systems - within the Mittagong road network in NSW.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Inspect, repair and replace damaged steel road safety barriers across the Mittagong road network, restoring guardrail and wire rope safety barrier systems to standard.
key_deliverables: Inspection of damaged steel safety barriers across the Mittagong road network
Repair and replacement of damaged guardrail systems
Repair and replacement of damaged wire rope safety barrier systems
tenders.scope_of_work: The works comprise the inspection, repair and replacement of damaged steel safety barriers, including guardrail and wire rope safety barrier systems, within the Mittagong road network.
tenders.reasoning: Discovered via buy.nsw / Transport for NSW search; the scope text is quoted from the buy.nsw notice and corroborated across two independent searches. Buyer-originated (Transport for NSW on buy.nsw / NSW eTendering).
tenders.notes: Headline-level only. No RFT ID could be recovered and the cited closing date of 3 August 2026 appeared in only one snippet and could not be verified (detail page HTTP 403). Pending a detail pass to confirm ID, closing date and evaluation criteria.

discovered_sources.source_name: DECnet - Defence Estate Community (Defence estate works ATMs/EOIs)
discovered_sources.how_discovered: While verifying the AusTender Defence estate EOI ESTS4000PH6, its full public listing (publish/close dates, scope, evaluation criteria, contact) surfaced on DECnet - a public community/notice channel for Australia's Defence estate community where the Department of Defence and its managing contractors (e.g. RPS Consulting) publish estate capital-works ATMs and EOIs.
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-adjacent channel for Department of Defence estate/construction works, useful for facilities opportunities that are hard to identify from AusTender rows alone. Cross-check individual notices against AusTender. Not a paywalled subscription aggregator.

discovered_sources.source_name: Snowy Monaro Regional Council (NSW) - Tenders & Contracts (VendorPanel)
discovered_sources.how_discovered: Surfaced via a search for NSW council plant & equipment hire panels; the council runs its quotes and panels through the VendorPanel procurement platform (free supplier registration), with procurement contact 1300 345 345 / council@smrc.nsw.gov.au.
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated local-government channel (VendorPanel). Not previously in the known-source list.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Surfaced via a search for current NSW council tenders/EOIs; Penrith City Council publishes current tenders on its own council-business tenders page.
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated local-government tender page. Not previously in the known-source list.
